original_hypothesis.title: Cross-contract/cross-chain replay of payerSignature in close() due to lack of domain binding
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The payerSignature in close() signs keccak256('CLOSE', channelId, cumulativeAmount) under personal_sign without including address(this) or chainId. If a matching channelId exists on another deployment or chain, the same payerSignature could be replayed to authorize a cooperative close there (voucher signature is EIP-712 bound, but payerSignature is not).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Replay vulnerabilities become practical when protocols deploy the same contracts across multiple environments (testnet/mainnet, L2s) and users reuse keys; even if channelId collisions are unlikely, operational processes may inadvertently reproduce them (same payer/payee/token/timestamp/counter on fresh deployments).
deployment_assumptions: Same payer key is used across multiple chains or multiple TempoStreamChannel deployments
A target deployment contains a channel with the same channelId and cumulativeAmount context (collision feasible via replicated channel creation conditions or deliberate engineering)
Attacker can obtain payerSignature from one environment
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: close() constructs closeHash without address(this) or chainId; no deployment constraints provided to prevent multi-chain reuse.

proof_of_signal.pattern: closeHash = keccak256(abi.encodePacked('CLOSE', voucher.channelId, voucher.cumulativeAmount)); closeDigest = keccak256('\x19Ethereum Signed Message:\n32', closeHash) (no chainId/contract)

preconditions: Attacker obtains payerSignature for CLOSE from environment A
Environment B has a channel with matching channelId and cumulativeAmount semantics
Attacker also has an authorizedSigner voucher signature valid on environment B (or can obtain it)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Dual signature scheme mixes EIP-712 (domain bound) with personal_sign (not domain bound)
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Across deployments/chains where users reuse keys

operational_questions: Will TempoStreamChannel be deployed to multiple chains/instances with the same name/version?
Are channelIds ever mirrored between environments (e.g., deterministic counter resets on redeploy)?
Does the client include environment binding in what it asks users to sign, despite on-chain not enforcing it?
validation_requirements: Attempt replay of payerSignature across two local deployments with engineered channelId match
Assess feasibility of channelId collision (timestamp/counter controllability in deployment migrations)
Confirm whether authorizedSigner signatures remain non-replayable due to EIP-712 domain separation

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Collect payerSignature from a cooperative close in one environment
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Engineer/locate a matching channelId on a second deployment (requires aligning openChannel inputs and counter/timestamp conditions)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Provide corresponding voucher signature for environment B
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Submit close() on environment B using replayed payerSignature
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: High; requires signature collection + environment/channelId alignment

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Unauthorized cooperative close authorizations across environments; legal/accounting disputes
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium (can be mitigated by client signing policies; best fixed in contract)

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Depends on multi-environment deployments and signature reuse

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Similar channelIds across deployments; repeated cumulativeAmount close authorizations
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Users reporting they never authorized close on a given chain
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Identical payerSignature bytes observed across chains (requires off-chain data)

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
